title: nymphoides tonkinensis
intro: Nymphoides tonkinensis, also known as the fish-bellied water-lily or tonkin yellow water lily, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Menyanthaceae. It is traditionally used in Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the Nymphoides tonkinensis plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
